Questions.Question: What is the size of an int in C#?
Questions.Explanation: In C#, 'int' maps directly to System.Int32, which is always 32-bit (4 bytes) regardless of platform or architecture.

Questions.Question: Which keyword is used to declare a constant in C#?
Questions.Explanation: The 'const' keyword declares a compile-time constant, whereas 'readonly' declares a run-time constant.

Questions.Question: Which access modifier allows access within the same assembly or subclass?
Questions.Explanation: The 'protected internal' modifier allows access within the defining assembly, or from derived classes in any assembly.
